title: How our tools work
metaTitle: How our tools work | ConvertBase.app Methodology
metaDescription: Learn where ConvertBase.app formulas, standards and algorithms come from and how we maintain accuracy.
heading: How our tools work
content: Every tool on ConvertBase.app is based on publicly available formulas, standards, or algorithms. Here's where our data comes from and how we maintain accuracy.

sections.title: Unit converters
paragraphs: Our unit conversion tools use conversion constants compliant with the International System of Units (SI) and official definitions of imperial units. Sources: BIPM, NIST.

sections.title: Health calculators (BMI, calories)
paragraphs: The BMI calculator uses the Quetelet formula (weight in kg / height in m²) as defined by the World Health Organisation (WHO). Category ranges follow WHO 2000 guidelines.

sections.title: Financial calculators
paragraphs: Loan and instalment calculations use standard financial mathematics formulas (equal instalments - annuity method; decreasing instalments - capital method). These tools are for informational purposes only and do not constitute financial advice.

sections.title: Construction calculators
paragraphs: Material estimates are based on standard industry norms and coefficients used in construction practice. We always recommend adding a waste margin - the suggested percentage is shown next to each calculator.

sections.title: Developer tools
paragraphs: All data operations (Base64 encoding, hashing, JSON formatting) use algorithms defined by IETF, W3C, or NIST. All operations run locally in your browser - no data is sent to our servers.

sections.title: Reporting errors
paragraphs: Spotted a calculation error or an outdated formula? Contact us - every report is reviewed and fixed.
